Assert exactly one visible toast whose message reflects the latest progress (output) and that the app shell render counter stayed flat (side-effect). Push 10 distinct toasts with max=4 and assert 4 visible + 6 queued; dismiss one and assert a queued toast promotes. Chaos test: interleave pushes and dismisses 1,000 times and assert visible never exceeds max and no timer leaks (timers map empties when all dismissed).
what_breaks_naive_approach: Throttling setToasts drops intermediate progress updates and still stores toasts in the shell-wide context, so the shell re-renders on every surviving push and a code path that resets the context for the sidebar toggle wipes the in-flight toast queue.

title: Migrate prop-drilled selection state to a context + reducer for a 1,000-item multi-select data grid
business_context: An ESG reporting grid drills selectedIds and a setter through 8 wrapper layers to every row's checkbox. Selecting all 1,000 rows re-renders all 8 wrapper layers and all 1,000 rows because the Set is recreated and drilled. Shift-click range selection is buggy because the anchor index lives in a different component than the selection set, so ranges sometimes select the wrong span.
constraints: Selecting a row must re-render only that row, not all 1,000
Select-all / range-select must be O(1) dispatch, not O(rows) prop updates
Shift-click anchor and selection set must live together for correct ranges
Selection must survive sort/filter (keyed by id, not index)
No prop drilling of selection through wrapper layers

before_state.description: selectedIds Set and setter drilled through 8 layers; anchor index separated from the set causes wrong ranges.

issues: selected/setSelected drilled through 8 wrappers
new Set drilled -> all rows re-render on any selection
anchor separated from selection -> wrong shift-click ranges
Reads the whole selected Set, re-renders when any row toggles
Range logic split between anchor and selected

reasoning: Naive approach: memoize Row with React.memo. Rejected because every Row receives the same selected Set prop which changes identity on any toggle, so memo never prevents re-render. Alternative: store selection in a Zustand store, rejected to avoid adding a dependency for a single grid; context+reducer suffices. Chosen: a SelectionContext backed by a reducer holding { selected:Set, anchor } so range logic is colocated; rows read membership via a useIsSelected(id) hook that subscribes narrowly (split context: dispatch context stable + a per-id selector). Trace: checkbox -> dispatch TOGGLE/RANGE -> reducer updates Set + anchor together -> useIsSelected(id) re-renders only that row -> selection keyed by id survives sort -> tests assert single-row re-render and correct ranges.

after_state.description: A selection reducer colocating anchor with the Set; rows subscribe per-id so only the toggled row re-renders.

files.purpose: Colocates anchor with the selection set so shift-click ranges compute correctly against the current order.
Splits dispatch (stable) from state so a row reads only its own membership and re-renders narrowly.
No drilled props; the row dispatches and reads its own selection state, so toggling one row re-renders only it.

migration_strategy: Add SelectionProvider behind GRID_SELECTION flag; keep drilled props as fallback
Migrate Row to useIsSelected/useSelectionDispatch; profile that toggling one row re-renders one row
Move shift-click range to the RANGE action and verify correct spans across sort/filter
Ramp to all reports; confirm select-all is one dispatch, not 1,000 prop updates
Remove drilled selection props from the 8 wrapper layers
testing_strategy: Fixture: a 1,000-row grid; toggle row 500 and assert only row 500 re-rendered (side-effect via per-row render counters) and selected contains its id (output). Set anchor at row 100, shift-click row 110 against the ordered id list, and assert exactly rows 100-110 selected. Re-sort the grid and assert selection (by id) persists. Chaos test: 2,000 random toggles, ranges, and select-alls interleaved with re-sorts and assert the selected Set always equals the set of ids the actions implied.
what_breaks_naive_approach: React.memo on Row can't help because all 1,000 rows receive the same selected Set prop, whose identity changes on every toggle — so a single checkbox click re-renders every row and all 8 wrapper layers that forward the Set.

title: Externalize a presence/typing-indicator store so it doesn't churn the message list in a chat app
business_context: A team chat shows presence dots and typing indicators sourced from ephemeral events at up to 20/sec in a busy channel. They're stored in the same state slice as messages, so every presence ping re-renders the virtualized 2,000-message list. Scroll position jumps and the list re-measures, making reading mid-history impossible during active chats.
constraints: Presence/typing updates must not re-render the message list
Typing indicators must auto-expire after 3s of no events
Presence must reflect the latest event per user (not accumulate)
Must handle 20 events/sec without main-thread jank
Message list virtualization measurements must stay stable

before_state.description: Presence events update the same store slice as messages, re-rendering the message list on every ping.

issues: presence/typing live with messages -> list re-renders on every ping
No expiry for typing indicators
Components subscribe to whole store
Reads the store; presence changes cause re-render and re-measure
Scroll position jumps on presence pings

reasoning: Naive approach: select only messages in MessageList. Rejected as insufficient: presence still lives in the heavy store and other components over-subscribe; we also want auto-expiry that fits an external store better. Alternative: keep presence in React with throttling, rejected because throttling delays presence and still re-renders. Chosen: a separate presenceStore (outside React) keyed by user holding latest timestamp, with an internal sweep that expires typing after 3s; only the presence bar subscribes via useSyncExternalStore. The message list reads a messages-only selector untouched by presence. Trace: presence event -> presenceStore.update outside React -> sweep expires typing -> only presence bar re-renders -> message list stable -> tests assert list render count flat under a presence storm.

after_state.description: A dedicated presence store with auto-expiry; the message list never re-renders on presence events.

files.purpose: Holds latest-per-user presence and typing with a 3s sweep, fully isolated from message state.
Only presence-displaying components subscribe; the message list is decoupled entirely.
Reads a messages-only selector so presence storms never trigger a re-render or re-measure.

migration_strategy: Add presenceStore behind PRESENCE_STORE flag; move presence/typing actions off the channel store
Migrate the presence bar to usePresence and the message list to a messages-only selector
Verify a 20/sec presence storm leaves the message list render count flat in canary
Enable the 3s typing sweep and confirm indicators clear without extra events
Remove presence/typing from the channel store after scroll-jump reports stop
testing_strategy: Fixture: a 2,000-message virtualized list plus a presence storm of 20 pings/sec for 5s with fake timers. Assert the message list render counter stays at 1 (side-effect) while the presence bar updates the online set (output). Set a user typing and advance 3s and assert they're removed from the typing snapshot. Chaos test: 1,000 interleaved pings and typing events and assert presence holds only the latest timestamp per user and the message list never re-measured.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Selecting only messages in MessageList fixes that one component, but presence still lives in the channel store, so every other presence-reading component over-subscribes; and without auto-expiry, typing indicators stick forever when a user stops typing without sending a 'stopped' event.

title: Replace a Context.Consumer render-prop tree with useContextSelector to fix nested re-renders in a builder
business_context: A form-builder app uses a single FormContext with nested <Context.Consumer> render props 6 levels deep. Editing one field's label re-renders the entire builder canvas (300 field nodes) because Consumer re-renders on any context value change. The team is on React 17 patterns and the value object is recreated each render.
constraints: Editing one field must re-render only that field's node
Must work without upgrading off the current Context API wholesale
Value identity churn must not cascade to all consumers
Selecting a slice must support custom equality
300-node canvas must stay at 60fps during rapid label edits

before_state.description: Nested Context.Consumer render props re-render on any context change; the value object churns each render.

issues: value recreated each render -> all consumers re-render
Consumer-based subscription has no selector
Consumer re-renders whenever any field changes
Reads whole fields map, not just this id

reasoning: Naive approach: wrap FieldNode in React.memo. Rejected because Context.Consumer subscribes to the whole value and bypasses memo on context change. Alternative: split into per-field contexts (300 providers), rejected as impractical for a dynamic field count. Chosen: use the use-context-selector library (a drop-in createContext with useContextSelector) so each FieldNode subscribes only to fields[id] with equality; the provider value is memoized. This keeps the Context mental model while gaining selector granularity without a full state-lib migration. Trace: edit -> setFields updates one field -> useContextSelector(ctx, s => s.fields[id]) only re-renders that node -> equality skips unrelated -> 300 nodes stay idle -> tests assert single-node re-render.

after_state.description: use-context-selector gives per-field subscriptions with equality, so editing one field re-renders only its node.

files.purpose: Selector-aware context with a memoized value so identity churn no longer cascades to consumers.
Subscribes only to fields[id] and the stable setter, so other fields' edits don't re-render this node.
Reads its own field via selector; editing field A leaves all other field nodes untouched.

migration_strategy: Add use-context-selector and the selector hook behind CTX_SELECTOR flag; keep Consumer tree as fallback
Migrate FieldNode to useField; profile re-renders when editing one label on a 300-field form
Verify equality prevents re-render when an unrelated field updates in canary
Ramp to all builders; confirm 60fps during rapid label edits
Remove the Context.Consumer render props after render-count dashboards are flat
testing_strategy: Fixture: a 300-field form with per-node render counters. Edit field 150's label and assert only node 150 re-rendered (side-effect) and its label value updated (output). Edit an unrelated field and assert node 150's counter is unchanged. Chaos test: drive 1,000 label edits across random fields and assert each edit re-renders exactly one node; verify a no-op set (same label) triggers zero re-renders via the selector equality.
what_breaks_naive_approach: React.memo can't stop a Context.Consumer from re-rendering because Consumer subscribes directly to context updates and ignores the memo boundary — so any field edit recreates the value object and re-renders all 300 nodes inside their Consumers.

title: Introduce useOptimistic + useActionState for an inventory adjustment form in a Next.js 15 app
business_context: A warehouse app's stock-adjust form (Next.js 15 app router, Server Actions) shows a spinner for ~700ms while the server action commits an adjustment, during which pickers can't see the new count and sometimes submit a second adjustment. The form also loses the entered delta on validation error because state resets on the action round-trip.
constraints: New count must show optimistically while the server action is pending
On server validation error, the entered delta must be preserved
Concurrent submits for the same SKU must be prevented while pending
Must use React 19 useOptimistic/useActionState, not client useState mirrors
Server remains source of truth; optimistic value reconciles or reverts

before_state.description: Server-action form shows a spinner and resets fields on error; no optimistic count, double-submits possible.

issues: No optimistic count -> spinner for 700ms
Field resets on error round-trip
No guard against double submit

reasoning: Naive approach: mirror count in useState and setCount on submit. Rejected because it duplicates server truth and React 19 provides purpose-built hooks; the mirror also doesn't auto-revert on error. Alternative: a client-side optimistic store, rejected as unnecessary given useOptimistic integrates with the action lifecycle and pending state. Chosen: useOptimistic to derive the projected count from the pending delta, useActionState to retain form state and surface validation errors (preserving the delta), and the form's pending state to disable the button. Trace: submit -> useOptimistic projects count+delta immediately -> server action commits -> on success the new server count flows back -> on error useActionState returns the prior input + message, count reverts -> pending disables re-submit -> tests assert optimistic display and revert.

after_state.description: useOptimistic projects the count instantly and useActionState preserves input and reverts on error.

files.purpose: Server action validates and commits; on error it echoes the entered delta so the client can preserve it.
Projects the new count instantly via useOptimistic, preserves the delta on error via useActionState, and disables submit while pending.
Single authoritative write returning the reconciled count that replaces the optimistic projection.

migration_strategy: Add the server action + hooks behind OPTIMISTIC_ADJUST flag; keep the spinner form as fallback
Enable for one warehouse; verify the count updates instantly and double-submit is blocked by pending
Trigger a validation error in staging and confirm the delta input is preserved
Ramp to all warehouses; watch duplicate-adjustment rate
Remove the legacy spinner form after duplicate adjustments drop to zero
testing_strategy: Fixture: render with count=100; submit delta=5 and assert the displayed count becomes 105 before the action resolves (output: optimistic) and the button is disabled while pending (side-effect). Submit an invalid delta='abc' and assert the action returns an error, the input retains 'abc', and the count reverts to 100. Chaos test: fire two submits rapidly and assert only one action runs while pending; simulate the server returning 103 (concurrent external adjustment) and assert the reconciled count overrides the optimistic projection.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A useState mirror of count doesn't auto-revert when the server action rejects, so a failed adjustment leaves the UI showing the optimistic count permanently, and because the mirror isn't tied to the action's pending lifecycle, nothing disables the button to stop a double submit.

title: Split a monolithic editor context into document/selection/collaboration stores for a collaborative doc
business_context: A collaborative rich-text editor keeps document content, cursor selection, and remote-collaborator presence in one EditorContext. Remote cursor movements (10/sec per collaborator, up to 8 collaborators = 80/sec) re-render the entire document, dropping typing to 25fps. Local selection changes also re-render remote-presence avatars unnecessarily, and an undo accidentally reverts remote presence because it's in the same state tree.
constraints: Remote cursor updates must not re-render the document content
Local selection must not re-render collaborator avatars
Document content, selection, and presence must be independently undoable/serializable
80 presence events/sec must not affect typing fps
CRDT document state must remain the single source of truth for content

before_state.description: One EditorContext bundles content, selection, and presence; any presence tick re-renders the document.

issues: Presence updates re-render the document (in the same value)
Local selection re-renders avatars
Undo over the bundled state reverts presence too

reasoning: Naive approach: memoize the document component. Rejected because it reads the shared context whose value changes on every presence tick. Alternative: keep one context but use useContextSelector, rejected because content/presence have different lifecycles (CRDT vs ephemeral) and belong in separate stores with separate persistence. Chosen: three stores — a CRDT-backed documentStore (content), a selectionStore (local), and a presenceStore (ephemeral remote) — each exposed via useSyncExternalStore; presence ticks never touch the document. Trace: remote cursor -> presenceStore.update outside React -> only avatar layer re-renders -> local selection -> selectionStore -> caret layer only -> document edits via CRDT -> undo scoped to document -> tests assert document render count flat under presence storm.

after_state.description: Three independent stores (document CRDT, selection, presence); presence ticks never re-render the document.

files.purpose: Ephemeral remote-cursor store isolated from document content so presence churn can't re-render text.
CRDT document as the single content source; the only re-render trigger for the text layer.
Each layer subscribes to its own store, so presence and selection re-render independently of the document.

migration_strategy: Introduce the three stores behind EDITOR_SPLIT flag; bridge legacy context to them read-only
Migrate the document layer to documentStore; profile typing fps during an 80-event presence storm
Move presence to presenceStore and verify avatar updates don't re-render text in canary
Scope undo to the document store and confirm undo no longer reverts presence
Remove the bundled EditorContext after fps and undo-correctness checks pass
testing_strategy: Fixture: an 8-collaborator session emitting 80 cursor events/sec for 5s plus local typing. Assert the document layer render counter tracks only text edits, not presence (side-effect), while presence avatars update (output). Trigger an undo and assert remote presence is unchanged. Chaos test: interleave 1,000 presence updates, local selections, and document edits and assert each layer re-renders only on its own store's changes and undo affects only document content.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Memoizing the document component is defeated because it reads the shared EditorContext value, which is recreated on every one of the 80 presence ticks per second — so the document re-renders constantly and typing drops to 25fps regardless of the memo.

title: Move feature-flag evaluation out of render into a memoized store to stop flag-driven render thrash
business_context: A gaming platform evaluates 60 feature flags inline in components via evaluateFlag(user, context) calls during render. A context change (e.g., region detection resolving) re-evaluates all flags in every component, and the lobby (200 components) re-renders twice. Some flag evals do regex matching on user attributes, costing ~40ms per lobby render and causing matchmaking button lag.
constraints: Flag evaluation must be memoized per (flagKey, evaluation context), not run per render
A context change must re-evaluate only affected flags, not all 60 everywhere
Flags must be readable via a hook with stable identity for unchanged values
Evaluation must support gradual rollout (percent + targeting rules)
Must support overriding flags in QA without redeploy

before_state.description: Flags are evaluated inline in render; context changes re-run all evaluations across all components.

issues: evaluateFlag runs on every render of every consumer
Regex matching repeated per render
No memoization across the 60 flags

reasoning: Naive approach: useMemo around evaluateFlag in each component. Rejected because deps include context which changes, busting all memos, and the same flag is evaluated redundantly in many components. Alternative: precompute all flags in a top-level effect and pass via context, rejected because it re-renders everything when any flag changes and runs after paint. Chosen: a flagStore that evaluates lazily and caches by (flagKey, contextHash), invalidating only flags whose targeting depends on the changed context dimension; components read via useSyncExternalStore so identical results keep referential identity. QA overrides write to the store directly. Trace: context change -> store invalidates dependent flags only -> consumers re-render only for changed flags -> regex runs once per (flag, context) -> overrides bypass eval -> tests assert eval count and selective invalidation.

after_state.description: A memoized flag store evaluates once per (flag, context) and invalidates only flags affected by a context change.

files.purpose: Caches evaluations by flag, invalidates only flags whose targeting deps changed, and supports QA overrides.
Reads a single flag with stable identity; unchanged flags don't re-render their consumers.
Consumes one flag via the cached store; a region-context change re-evaluates only region-dependent flags.

migration_strategy: Add flagStore behind FLAG_STORE flag; route useFlag through it while keeping inline eval as fallback
Migrate the lobby first; verify a region-context resolve re-evaluates only region-dependent flags in canary
Confirm regex-heavy flags evaluate once per (flag, context) via an eval counter
Wire QA override panel to store.override and ramp to all surfaces
Remove inline evaluateFlag calls and add a lint ban on evaluating flags in render
testing_strategy: Fixture: 60 flags (10 region-dependent) and a lobby of 200 components; instrument evaluate with a counter. Change the region context once and assert only the 10 region-dependent flags re-evaluate (output: counter delta = 10) and only their consumers re-render (side-effect). Apply a QA override and assert get() returns the override without calling evaluate. Chaos test: change unrelated context dimensions 500 times and assert zero region-flag re-evaluations; assert two components reading the same unchanged flag get a referentially stable value.
what_breaks_naive_approach: useMemo in each component lists the whole context object as a dependency, so a region resolve busts every flag memo in all 200 components, re-running all 60 evaluations — including the expensive regex matches — exactly as if there were no memoization.

title: Convert a setInterval-driven countdown shared across components into a single store with derived remaining time
business_context: A flash-sale page shows the same countdown in the header, the product card, and a sticky CTA. Each runs its own setInterval(1000) and computes remaining = end - now, so the three clocks drift by up to a second and tick at different moments. When 50 product cards render in a grid, that's 50+ intervals firing setState every second, re-rendering the whole grid.
constraints: One ticking source; all countdowns must show the identical second
50+ cards must not each run a timer or re-render the grid every tick
Remaining time must be derived, not stored, to avoid drift
Must pause/resume accurately across tab visibility changes
Sale-ended transition must fire exactly once across all consumers

before_state.description: Each countdown component runs its own interval and local state; clocks drift and the grid re-renders per tick.

issues: One interval per Countdown instance -> 50+ timers
setNow per instance re-renders each independently and drifts
No coordinated sale-ended event

reasoning: Naive approach: lift one interval to a context and broadcast 'now'. Rejected because broadcasting now via context re-renders all consumers every second even those whose displayed second didn't change. Alternative: requestAnimationFrame loop, rejected as overkill for second-granularity and worse for battery. Chosen: a single clockStore ticking once per second (visibility-aware), exposing getNow; each countdown derives remaining via a selector that returns the formatted second, and useSyncExternalStore with equality so a card re-renders only when its displayed second changes; a one-shot ended flag handles the transition. Trace: clockStore ticks -> selectors compute remaining seconds -> equality skips unchanged -> grid cards re-render only on second boundaries relevant to them -> ended fires once -> tests assert single timer and synchronized display.

after_state.description: A single visibility-aware clock store; countdowns derive remaining seconds with equality so unchanged cards stay idle.

files.purpose: One ref-counted, visibility-aware interval driving a shared 'now' so every countdown ticks in lockstep.
Derives remaining seconds from the shared clock with equality so a card re-renders only on its second change.
Reads the shared derived second; all instances display the identical value with no per-card timer.

migration_strategy: Add clockStore behind SHARED_CLOCK flag; keep per-instance intervals as fallback
Migrate header/card/CTA countdowns to useCountdown; verify they show the same second in canary
Confirm a 50-card grid runs exactly one interval via a timer counter
Validate visibility pause/resume keeps the displayed second correct after tab switch
Remove per-instance intervals once drift reports stop
testing_strategy: Fixture: 50 cards plus header/CTA sharing one endsAt, with fake timers. Advance 1s and assert exactly one interval fired and all 51 countdowns show the identical value (output) while the grid re-rendered only the second-changing cards (side-effect). Drive a card whose second didn't change and assert it didn't re-render. Chaos test: toggle visibility hidden for 10s then visible and assert the displayed remaining jumps to the correct value with one catch-up tick; assert the sale-ended branch renders once at remaining 0.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Broadcasting a shared 'now' through context still re-renders every consumer each second because the context value changes every tick — even cards whose displayed second is identical re-render — so a 50-card grid re-renders fully once per second.

title: Replace deeply nested useState objects with a normalized entity store for a CRM contact graph
business_context: A CRM holds accounts->contacts->deals in nested useState objects. Updating a deal status requires spreading 3 levels (setAccounts(a => a.map(...contacts.map(...deals.map...)))), which is error-prone and re-renders every account row. A bug in the spread caused 600 deals to attach to the wrong account during a bulk status update because an index was reused after a filter.
constraints: Entities must be normalized (by id) so an update touches one record
Updating a deal must re-render only that deal's row
Relationships (account->contacts->deals) must be derivable without nesting
Bulk updates must be atomic and not corrupt relationships
Selectors must return stable references for unchanged entities

before_state.description: Deeply nested useState objects require multi-level spreads that re-render everything and corrupt relations on bulk edits.

issues: 3-level spread to update one deal
Whole accounts tree replaced -> all rows re-render
Bulk edits reuse indices after filter -> wrong-account attachment

reasoning: Naive approach: use immer to simplify the nested update. Rejected because immer eases the write but the tree is still one object whose change re-renders all consumers, and relationship corruption from index reuse remains possible. Alternative: a full normalized cache via RTK Entity Adapter, rejected to avoid a Redux migration; a small normalized store suffices. Chosen: normalize into byId maps (accounts, contacts, deals) plus relationship index arrays; updates patch a single entity; selectors join ids to assemble views and memoize per id for stable references. Trace: updateDeal -> patch deals byId[dealId] -> only that deal's subscribers re-render -> relationship indices stay intact (no index reuse) -> bulk update maps over ids transactionally -> tests assert single-record update and relationship integrity.

after_state.description: A normalized byId entity store with relationship indices; updating one deal touches one record and keeps relations intact.

files.purpose: Normalized maps with relationship indices; updates patch one entity and notify only its subscribers.
Subscribes to one deal id so updating it re-renders only that deal row.
Reads and updates a single normalized deal; no nested spreads and no cross-account corruption.

migration_strategy: Add the normalized store behind CRM_NORMALIZED flag; build it from the nested state at mount
Migrate DealRow to useDeal/updateDeal; verify updating one deal re-renders one row in canary
Move bulk status update to store.bulkUpdate and assert no relationship index is mutated
Ramp to all tenants; reconcile normalized data against the nested source in shadow mode
Remove nested useState and the multi-level spreads after integrity checks pass
testing_strategy: Fixture: 100 accounts, 1,000 contacts, 5,000 deals normalized. Update deal D's status and assert only D's row re-rendered (side-effect via per-row counters) and getDeal(D).status changed (output). Run a 600-deal bulk status update and assert each deal's contact/account relationship (via dealsByContact) is unchanged. Chaos test: interleave 2,000 single and bulk updates with reads and assert no deal's parent linkage ever changes and selectors return stable references for untouched deals.
what_breaks_naive_approach: immer simplifies the nested write but still produces a new top-level accounts object on every update, so all account rows re-render; and because the bulk path maps over filtered arrays by index, a reused index after filtering reattaches deals to the wrong account — exactly the corruption immer cannot prevent.

title: Adopt useTransition + a deferred-search store to keep input responsive over a 100k-record client search
business_context: A logistics shipment search filters 100,000 client-cached shipments on each keystroke synchronously, blocking the input for ~220ms per character so typing feels laggy and characters drop. The results list and the input both live in the same component, so the expensive filter runs in the same render that updates the input value.
constraints: Typing must stay responsive (input updates immediately)
Filtering 100k records must not block keystrokes
Stale filter results from a superseded query must be discarded
Must show a 'pending' state during heavy filtering
Search must be cancelable when a newer query arrives

before_state.description: The filter runs synchronously in the same render as the input update, blocking keystrokes.

issues: Filter runs in the same synchronous render as the input update
100k scan blocks the keystroke
No way to discard stale results

reasoning: Naive approach: debounce the query. Rejected because debounce just delays the block; once it runs it still freezes the input for 220ms and feels sluggish. Alternative: move filtering to a Web Worker, rejected as heavier than needed when React 18 concurrency can keep input responsive. Chosen: split state so the input value updates urgently while the filtered results update inside startTransition (low priority); useDeferredValue feeds the heavy list, and React discards superseded transitions automatically. A small store memoizes the last query+result for stale discard. Trace: keystroke -> setQ urgent (input responsive) -> startTransition updates query that drives the deferred filter -> React interrupts/discards stale transitions -> pending flag shows spinner -> tests assert input never blocks and stale results dropped.

after_state.description: useTransition + useDeferredValue keep the input urgent while the 100k filter runs at low priority and stale results drop.

files.purpose: Updates the input urgently and computes the heavy filter against a deferred query so keystrokes never block.
Input stays responsive; the list updates at low priority and shows a pending state during heavy filtering.
Memoized so the deferred results re-render the list only when results actually change.

migration_strategy: Add useShipmentSearch behind CONCURRENT_SEARCH flag; keep the synchronous filter as fallback
Migrate the search page; measure keystroke-to-paint latency in canary
Confirm superseded queries discard stale results via React's transition interruption
Ramp to all users; verify no dropped characters under fast typing on a 100k fixture
Remove the synchronous filter once input latency is under 50ms
testing_strategy: Fixture: 100,000 shipments; simulate typing 'ABC123' rapidly. Assert the input value reflects each keystroke immediately (output: input not blocked) and isPending is true while filtering (side-effect). Assert that when 'AB' is superseded by 'ABC' before the first filter completes, only 'ABC' results render (stale discard). Chaos test: type and delete 200 characters as fast as possible and assert no keystroke is dropped and the final results match the final query; verify the list memo prevents re-render when results are unchanged.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Debouncing the query only postpones the 220ms synchronous filter; when it eventually runs it still executes in a blocking render, freezing the input mid-typing — so fast typists still drop characters, just later.

title: Split a shopping-session context so a currency change stops re-rendering the recommendation carousel
business_context: A travel-booking site holds currency, locale, and a recommendation list in one SessionContext. When a user switches currency (12% of sessions do), the entire page including a recommendation carousel that runs intersection-observer animations re-renders, restarting the animations and re-fetching recommendation images. Conversion drops 4% on sessions where the carousel flickers.
constraints: Currency change must re-price displayed amounts without re-rendering the carousel
Recommendation list must not be re-fetched on currency change
Locale and currency must be independently switchable
Carousel animation state must survive a currency switch
Prices must recompute from a base currency, derived not stored

before_state.description: Currency, locale, and recommendations share one context; a currency switch re-renders and re-fetches everything.

issues: recs refetch effect keyed on currency -> images reload on switch
Single value re-renders carousel on currency change
Price conversion not separated from recommendation data

reasoning: Naive approach: remove currency from the recs effect deps. Rejected as a partial fix: the carousel still re-renders because it reads the shared context value that changes on currency switch. Alternative: useContextSelector on one context, rejected because recommendations and currency have different fetch lifecycles and deserve separate providers. Chosen: split into CurrencyContext, LocaleContext, and a RecommendationsContext whose fetch is keyed only on locale; prices derive via a usePrice(baseAmount) hook reading CurrencyContext rates. The carousel reads only RecommendationsContext, so a currency switch can't re-render or re-fetch it. Trace: currency switch -> CurrencyContext updates -> price components re-price via derived hook -> carousel (RecommendationsContext) untouched -> animations persist -> tests assert no recs refetch on currency change.

after_state.description: Three scoped contexts; prices derive from currency rates while the carousel reads only recommendations.

files.purpose: Isolated currency state with a derived usePrice hook so switching currency re-prices without touching other trees.
Recommendations fetch is keyed on locale only, so a currency switch never re-fetches images.
Reads recommendations from its own context; only the price labels re-render on a currency switch, not the carousel structure.

migration_strategy: Add the three providers behind SESSION_SPLIT flag, nested inside legacy SessionProvider
Move the recs fetch to a locale-keyed effect; verify a currency switch triggers no recs request in canary
Migrate the carousel to useRecommendations + usePrice and confirm animations persist on switch
Ramp to all sessions; watch carousel-flicker and conversion metrics
Remove SessionContext after the recs-refetch-on-currency request disappears from logs
testing_strategy: Fixture: a carousel with 12 recommendation cards and an intersection-observer animation flag. Switch currency and assert no /api/recs request fired (side-effect via fetch spy) and card prices re-priced via the rate (output). Assert the carousel's animation-started flag is preserved across the switch. Chaos test: toggle currency 100 times and assert recs fetch count stays at its initial value; switch locale and assert recs do re-fetch, proving the dependency split is correct.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Removing currency from the recs effect deps stops the re-fetch, but the carousel still consumes the combined SessionContext value, which is recreated on every currency change — so the carousel re-renders, its intersection-observer animations restart, and images flash.

title: Migrate a global event-bus + setState pattern to a reducer-backed store for an order-status board
business_context: A restaurant kitchen display uses a custom EventEmitter; components subscribe and call setState on 'order.updated' events firing ~30/min during rush. Each of the 6 station screens keeps its own order array, so a 'mark ready' on one screen doesn't reflect on others until a manual refresh. Race conditions between 'created' and 'updated' events occasionally show an order in two stations at once.
constraints: Single shared order state; all 6 stations reflect updates instantly
Event ordering must be deterministic (apply by sequence number)
An order must appear in exactly one station at a time
Optimistic 'mark ready' must reconcile with the server event
No per-screen divergent copies

before_state.description: Each screen subscribes to an event bus and maintains its own order array; screens diverge and races duplicate orders.

issues: Each station holds its own copy -> divergence
No sequence ordering -> created/updated race duplicates
mark ready not reflected across screens

reasoning: Naive approach: have the bus broadcast a shared array. Rejected because it still re-renders all stations on every event and doesn't fix event ordering. Alternative: socket.io rooms per station, rejected because the cross-station 'move order' use case needs a single coherent model, not per-room state. Chosen: a single reducer-backed orderStore applying events by sequence number so out-of-order created/updated converge; stations select their slice via useSyncExternalStore; 'mark ready' is optimistic and reconciled by the server event's sequence. Trace: event -> store.apply(seq, event) ignores stale seq -> single model updates -> stations select by station id -> optimistic mark ready -> server event reconciles -> tests assert single-station placement and convergence.

after_state.description: A sequence-ordered reducer store as the single order model; stations select their slice and stay in sync.

files.purpose: Single model applying events by sequence so created/updated races converge and an order has one station.
Each station reads its slice from the shared model, so a mark-ready on one screen reflects everywhere.
Renders the shared station slice; no per-screen copy, no divergence.

migration_strategy: Add orderStore + sequence application behind KDS_STORE flag; bridge bus events to store.apply
Migrate one station screen to useStation; verify mark-ready propagates to other screens in canary
Validate out-of-order created/updated events converge to one station via sequence ordering
Ramp to all stations; reconcile against the kitchen API to confirm no duplicate placement
Remove per-screen order arrays after divergence reports stop
testing_strategy: Fixture: a tape of order events including an 'updated' (seq 5) arriving before 'created' (seq 4) for the same order. Assert the store applies by sequence so the order ends in the correct single station (output) and that two station selectors never both contain it (side-effect). Mark an order ready optimistically and assert a stale server event (lower seq) doesn't revert it. Chaos test: replay 500 shuffled events across 6 stations and assert each order appears in exactly one station and all screens read the identical model.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Broadcasting a shared array from the bus still re-renders all 6 stations on every event and doesn't impose ordering, so a 'created' event arriving after its 'updated' re-adds an order that was already moved — placing it in two stations simultaneously.

title: Extract a wizard's derived-eligibility computation into a memoized selector to stop recompute on every keystroke
business_context: A mortgage pre-approval wizard recomputes a heavy eligibility model (DTI ratio, LTV, credit tier lookups) inline in render on every field change. The model does 30+ table lookups and takes ~90ms; typing income digit-by-digit recomputes it 6 times for '120000', janking the field. The recompute also runs when the user merely scrolls a dropdown because unrelated state changes trigger re-render.
constraints: Eligibility model must recompute only when its real inputs change
Typing in one field must not recompute on unrelated UI state changes
Result must be referentially stable when inputs are unchanged
Model must remain a pure function for audit/regulatory replay
Must support 'what-if' overrides without recomputing the base

before_state.description: The eligibility model runs inline in render on every state change, including unrelated UI updates.

issues: computeEligibility runs on every render, including dropdown open/close
6 recomputes for a 6-digit income entry
Recompute coupled to unrelated UI state

reasoning: Naive approach: useMemo(computeEligibility, [form]). Rejected because form is one object recreated on every field change, so unrelated field edits still recompute; also dropdown state lives in the same component so its toggle triggers the memo check unnecessarily and any non-form change re-renders. Alternative: move computation to a worker, rejected as the model must be synchronous for audit replay. Chosen: a pure selectEligibility(inputs) keyed on the specific fields it consumes via a memoized hook with a custom equality on those fields; UI-only state (dropdownOpen) is separated so it can't trigger recompute, and what-if overrides compose without touching the base memo. Trace: field change -> hook compares only model-relevant fields -> recompute only if they changed -> dropdown toggle recomputes nothing -> stable result reference -> tests assert recompute count.

after_state.description: A pure eligibility selector memoized on its real input fields; UI-only state can't trigger recompute.

files.purpose: Pure deterministic eligibility model suitable for regulatory replay; no React coupling.
Memoizes on the five model fields only, so unrelated edits and UI toggles don't recompute the 90ms model.
Reads the memoized eligibility; a dropdown toggle no longer recomputes the model.

migration_strategy: Add selectEligibility + useEligibility behind ELIG_SELECTOR flag; snapshot current outputs as a regression baseline
Migrate the wizard; profile recompute count while typing a 6-digit income
Verify dropdown toggles and unrelated UI changes recompute zero times in canary
Ramp to all applicants; confirm field input latency under 16ms
Remove inline computeEligibility and lint-ban model computation in render
testing_strategy: Fixture: instrument selectEligibility with a counter; type income '120000' digit by digit. Assert the model recomputed once per income change but not on dropdown toggles (output: counter delta), and that the result reference is stable when only dropdownOpen changes (side-effect: no recompute). Property test: 10k random inputs and assert approved iff dti<0.43 and ltv<=0.8 and tier!=='subprime'. Chaos test: 1,000 dropdown toggles interleaved with unrelated state and assert zero model recomputes.
what_breaks_naive_approach: useMemo(computeEligibility, [form]) keys on the whole form object, which is a new reference after every field edit, so editing any unrelated field — or toggling the dropdown which re-renders the component — still busts the memo and re-runs the 90ms model.

title: Build a useSyncExternalStore bridge over localStorage to sync auth state across browser tabs
business_context: A B2B dashboard stores the auth session in React state. When a user logs out in one tab, other open tabs keep showing authed UI and keep making API calls with the revoked token until refreshed, leaking 401s and occasionally showing another user's data after a re-login in a different tab. ~300 multi-tab sessions/day are affected.
constraints: Auth state must sync across tabs in real time via the storage event
Logout in any tab must immediately de-auth all tabs
Re-login as a different user must not leak the prior user's cached UI
Must work with React 18 and provide an SSR-safe snapshot
No polling; use the storage event

before_state.description: Auth lives in per-tab React state; a logout in one tab doesn't propagate, so other tabs stay authed.

issues: setSession updates only the current tab
Other tabs keep stale authed session
No storage-event listener -> no cross-tab sync

reasoning: Naive approach: poll localStorage on an interval. Rejected because it's laggy and wasteful and still races re-login. Alternative: BroadcastChannel, rejected as a fine option but the storage event already covers all tabs and is simpler/SSR-friendlier here; we want a single source synchronized by storage. Chosen: an authStore backed by localStorage that listens to the window 'storage' event and notifies subscribers, exposed via useSyncExternalStore so every tab re-reads on any change; logout removes the key and the storage event de-auths siblings, and a user-id check clears cached UI on identity change. Trace: logout -> remove key -> storage event in other tabs -> store re-reads null -> useSyncExternalStore re-renders all tabs unauthed -> re-login writes new session -> identity guard clears stale cache -> tests assert cross-tab de-auth.

after_state.description: An authStore synced via the storage event and useSyncExternalStore so all tabs share one session.

files.purpose: Single auth source synced across tabs via the storage event with an SSR-safe server snapshot.
Every tab re-renders on any auth change; logout in one tab de-auths all.
Clears cached UI/query data when the user identity changes, preventing cross-user data leaks on re-login.

migration_strategy: Add authStore + useAuth behind TAB_SYNC flag; keep per-tab useState as fallback
Migrate the auth provider; open two tabs and verify logout in one de-auths the other in canary
Add the IdentityGuard cache clear and verify re-login as a different user shows no stale data
Ramp to all users; monitor 401 rates from stale tokens in multi-tab sessions
Remove the per-tab useState auth after cross-tab de-auth is confirmed
testing_strategy: Fixture: two simulated tabs sharing a jsdom localStorage. Log in tab A, assert tab B's snapshot updates via a dispatched storage event (output). Log out in tab A and assert tab B's session becomes null and re-renders unauthed (side-effect). Re-login as a different userId and assert queryClient.clear was called (cache cleared). Chaos test: rapidly toggle login/logout across two tabs 200 times and assert both tabs always converge to the same session and never render authed after a logout.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Polling localStorage adds latency and a race window where, between two polls, a tab can fire an API call with a token already revoked in another tab — and on a fast re-login as a different user the poll-based read may briefly serve the previous user's cached UI.

title: Replace a 700-line reducer with combined slice reducers for a multi-tab spreadsheet app
business_context: A spreadsheet app has one 700-line reducer handling cells, formulas, selection, clipboard, and history. A single 'paste' action's case is 120 lines; the file is unmergeable in code review and a recent edit to the selection case accidentally broke formula recalculation because the cases share local variables. Adding a feature means touching the monolith and risking unrelated breakage.
constraints: Reducer must be split into independently testable slice reducers
A cell edit must not run selection/clipboard logic
Combined reducer must preserve current action semantics exactly
Cross-slice actions (paste affects cells + history) must be coordinated explicitly
History/undo must compose across slices

before_state.description: One 700-line reducer mixes five concerns; cases share locals and edits cause cross-concern breakage.

issues: 700-line single reducer mixing 5 concerns
Shared locals across cases cause cross-bugs
Cross-slice actions tangled inline

reasoning: Naive approach: split the file but keep one switch. Rejected because the concerns still share one state object and locals; the coupling remains. Alternative: Redux Toolkit createSlice with combineReducers, rejected because the app uses a plain useReducer and wants to avoid the store migration. Chosen: independent slice reducers (cellsReducer, selectionReducer, clipboardReducer) combined by a root reducer; cross-slice actions (PASTE) are handled by an explicit coordinator that calls the affected slice reducers in order and records one composite history entry. Trace: action -> root dispatches to relevant slice reducers -> EDIT_CELL touches only cells -> PASTE coordinator sequences cells+clipboard+history -> undo composes slice inverses -> tests assert slice isolation and exact semantics parity.

after_state.description: Combined slice reducers with an explicit coordinator for cross-slice actions and composed history.

files.purpose: Each concern is an isolated, independently testable reducer with no shared locals.
Combines slices and routes cross-slice actions to a dedicated coordinator instead of a tangled case.
Sequences the cell update and records one composite history entry, making paste's cross-slice effect explicit and undoable.

migration_strategy: Split the reducer into slices behind the same dispatch API; add a parity test asserting identical output vs the monolith
Migrate EDIT_CELL/SELECT/COPY to slices; run the spreadsheet test suite to confirm semantics
Move PASTE to the coordinator and verify cells+clipboard+history match the old behavior
Compose undo across slices and validate undo of a paste restores the prior cells
Delete the 700-line reducer after parity tests pass on the full action corpus
testing_strategy: Fixture: a recorded action log (edit, select, copy, paste, undo) from a real session. Replay through both the monolith and rootReducer and assert identical final state (output: parity). Assert an EDIT_CELL action leaves selection and clipboard referentially unchanged (side-effect: slice isolation). Assert undo of a paste restores the exact prior cells. Chaos test: fuzz 5,000 random action sequences and assert rootReducer never throws and the cells slice is only mutated by cell/paste actions, never by SELECT.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Splitting the file but keeping one switch leaves all five concerns sharing the single state object and the reused recalc local, so an edit to the SELECT case can still clobber the recompute path — the exact cross-concern bug that broke formula recalculation.

title: Introduce optimistic concurrency with version checks in a reducer to prevent lost updates on a shared note
business_context: A team wiki lets two editors save a note; the save reducer overwrites server content with local content, so when two people edit concurrently the last save silently discards the other's changes. Support sees ~15 'my edit disappeared' reports/week. There's no version awareness, so the client can't detect that the server moved on.
constraints: Saves must include a base version; the server rejects stale saves
On version conflict the client must surface a merge, not silently overwrite
Local edits must be preserved during a conflict
Optimistic save must reconcile the new server version on success
Must handle rapid successive saves without losing the version chain

before_state.description: The save reducer overwrites server content with local content with no version check, losing concurrent edits.

issues: No base version sent or checked
SAVED blindly overwrites -> lost concurrent edits
No conflict detection

reasoning: Naive approach: lock the note while editing. Rejected because pessimistic locking blocks the second editor and doesn't fit a wiki's collaborative feel. Alternative: full CRDT merge, rejected as too heavy for this milestone; optimistic concurrency with version checks solves the lost-update problem proportionately. Chosen: the reducer tracks baseVersion; saves send { baseVersion, content }; the server returns 409 with the latest server content when versions diverge, and the reducer enters a 'conflict' state holding both local and server content for the user to merge, never overwriting silently. Trace: edit -> reducer bumps dirty -> save sends baseVersion -> 200 reconciles new version, 409 -> CONFLICT preserving local + server -> user merges -> save again with new base -> tests assert stale save rejected and local preserved.

after_state.description: A version-aware reducer doing optimistic concurrency; conflicts surface a merge instead of overwriting.

files.purpose: Tracks version and represents a conflict explicitly, preserving local content instead of overwriting.
Sends baseVersion and routes a 409 into a conflict state, reconciling the new version on success.
Renders a merge view on conflict so the second editor's changes are never silently lost.

migration_strategy: Add version tracking and 409 handling behind NOTE_OCC flag; server starts returning versions and 409s
Migrate the editor; simulate two concurrent saves in canary and verify the second gets a conflict
Add the merge view and confirm local content is preserved through a conflict
Ramp to all wikis; watch 'edit disappeared' report volume
Remove the blind-overwrite SAVED path after lost-update reports drop to zero
testing_strategy: Fixture: server at version 7; client saves with baseVersion 6. Assert the reducer enters conflict with both local and server content (output) and that no SAVED overwrite occurred (side-effect). Save with the correct base and assert SAVED advances the version. Chaos test: simulate 100 interleaved concurrent saves from two clients and assert no save with a stale baseVersion is accepted and every conflict preserves the local text; verify rapid successive saves chain versions without skipping.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A blind SAVED overwrite has no version awareness, so when editor B saves after editor A advanced the server, B's reducer overwrites the note with B's content — A's changes vanish with no conflict ever detected.

title: Split a map app's viewport/markers/layers context to stop marker hover from re-rendering 5k polygons
business_context: A real-estate map keeps viewport, 5,000 markers, and vector layers in one MapContext. Hovering a marker (which sets a hoveredId in context) re-renders all 5,000 markers and the polygon layer, dropping hover responsiveness to ~150ms and making the heatmap layer flicker. Panning also re-renders markers even though their positions didn't change.
constraints: Marker hover must re-render only the hovered marker
Panning must not re-render markers whose data is unchanged
Vector layers must not re-render on marker hover
Hover state must be isolated from marker data
5,000 markers must hover at 60fps

before_state.description: Viewport, markers, and hover share one context; a hover re-renders all markers and layers.

issues: hoveredId in the shared value re-renders all markers on hover
Panning re-renders markers despite unchanged data
Layers re-render on hover

reasoning: Naive approach: memoize each Marker. Rejected because markers read the shared context value that changes on hover, defeating memo. Alternative: useContextSelector for hoveredId, rejected because viewport/markers/hover have distinct update frequencies and belong in separate providers for clean isolation. Chosen: split into ViewportContext, MarkersContext, and a HoverContext; markers read their data from MarkersContext and their hovered flag via a useIsHovered(id) selector subscribing only to HoverContext, so a hover re-renders only the entered and exited markers. Layers read MarkersContext only. Trace: hover -> HoverContext sets id -> useIsHovered re-renders the two affected markers -> markers/layers data untouched -> pan -> ViewportContext only -> tests assert two-marker re-render on hover.

after_state.description: Three scoped contexts; markers read a per-id hover selector so only entered/exited markers re-render.

files.purpose: Isolated hover state; markers query their own hovered boolean so a hover affects only two markers.
Marker data lives apart from hover and viewport, so neither hovering nor panning re-renders it.
Memoized on marker data and reads only its hover flag, so panning and unrelated hovers don't re-render it.

migration_strategy: Add the three providers behind MAP_SPLIT flag nested in legacy MapProvider
Migrate markers to useMarkers + useIsHovered; profile re-renders on a single hover
Verify panning re-renders zero markers when their data is unchanged in canary
Ramp to all users; confirm hover stays at 60fps on a 5,000-marker map
Remove MapContext after hover/pan render-count dashboards are flat
testing_strategy: Fixture: 5,000 markers with per-marker render counters. Hover marker 2500 and assert only it (and the previously hovered marker) re-rendered (side-effect) and its active flag is true (output). Pan the viewport and assert zero markers re-rendered. Chaos test: move the mouse across 1,000 markers in sequence and assert each hover transition re-renders exactly the entering and leaving markers; verify the polygon layer never re-renders on any hover.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Memoizing Marker fails because each marker consumes the shared MapContext whose value object changes whenever hoveredId updates, so a single hover invalidates context for all 5,000 markers and they all re-render regardless of the memo's prop comparison.

title: Move pagination + infinite-scroll accumulation from component state into a normalized query store
business_context: A jobs board accumulates infinite-scroll pages in a component useState array. Navigating away and back loses all loaded pages, refetching from page 1 (avg 8 pages re-fetched/session). Duplicate jobs appear because page boundaries shift when new jobs are posted between fetches, and the same job id renders twice, breaking the apply button's per-id state.
constraints: Loaded pages must persist across navigation (cache by query key)
Items must dedupe by id even when page boundaries shift
Returning to the list must restore scroll and loaded pages without refetch
Must support cursor-based pagination to avoid offset drift
New items must merge without duplicating existing ids

before_state.description: Pages accumulate in component useState; navigation loses them and offset paging duplicates jobs.

issues: pages in useState lost on navigation -> refetch from page 1
Offset paging drifts when new jobs posted -> duplicate ids
No dedupe by id

reasoning: Naive approach: cache pages in a module variable. Rejected as a fragile hand-rolled cache without invalidation or dedupe. Alternative: keep offset paging but dedupe on render, rejected because offset drift can also skip jobs, not just duplicate. Chosen: TanStack Query useInfiniteQuery with cursor-based pagination keyed by the filter query; a select() merges pages and dedupes by id into a normalized list; the cache persists across navigation so returning restores pages and scroll. Trace: scroll -> fetchNextPage with cursor -> query cache stores pages keyed by filters -> select dedupes by id -> navigation keeps cache -> return restores instantly -> tests assert no refetch on return and no duplicate ids.

after_state.description: Cursor-based useInfiniteQuery with id-dedupe select; the cache persists pages across navigation.

files.purpose: Cursor pagination cached by filters with a select that dedupes by id, eliminating offset-drift duplicates.
Reads the deduped, cached job list; navigating away and back restores pages without refetch.
Restores scroll position alongside the cached pages so returning to the list feels instant.

migration_strategy: Add useJobs (cursor query) behind JOBS_QUERY flag; keep offset useState path as fallback
Migrate the list; verify navigating away and back restores pages with no refetch in canary
Switch the API to cursor pagination and confirm the dedupe select removes boundary duplicates
Ramp to all users; watch page-1 refetch volume and duplicate-card reports drop
Remove the offset useState accumulation after dedupe and restore are verified
testing_strategy: Fixture: a cursor API returning overlapping pages (a job posted between fetches appears on two pages). Load three pages and assert the deduped list has no repeated ids (output). Navigate away and back and assert queryFn fired zero additional times (side-effect via fetch spy). Chaos test: interleave new-job insertions with scrolling and assert no job id renders twice and no job is skipped; verify scroll position restores within a pixel on return.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A module-variable cache has no per-filter keying or id-dedupe, so switching filters serves stale jobs and the offset-based fetch still returns overlapping pages after a new job is posted — the same job id renders twice and breaks the per-id apply button.
